categories.title: Ruby Trivia Token and Developer Activity Discussion

content.text: Community members discussed the Ruby Trivia project and its $TRIVIA token, which launched on Solana as part of a multi-game trivia platform featuring server-scored games, live and async multiplayer, mobile support, and eight languages. Holding $TRIVIA provides an in-game score multiplier. An official Telegram channel was announced as live. One user repeatedly questioned whether the token was dead due to low chat and Twitter activity, which prompted a terse response from another community member. Despite concerns about low activity and mentions of ongoing lawsuits, other members expressed optimism, with one predicting the project could reach a 10 million valuation. A core developer acknowledged the need to post screenshots to demonstrate progress, and another member noted that GitHub activity was still ongoing. The Ruby Trivia AI account also announced an upcoming Twitter Space. Separately, in the coders channel, two developers posted availability notices, one looking for work and another offering development services via direct message.

categories.title: ElizaOS Major Architectural Update: Gemma 4 Migration and Pipeline Hardening

content.text: On June 25, 2026, the ElizaOS ecosystem completed a significant architectural transition, migrating core training, reinforcement learning, and local inference defaults from Qwen to Gemma 4 across all model sizes including E2B, E4B, 12B, and 31B variants. This migration ensures full compatibility with Eliza-1 release tiers and represents a foundational shift in the model ecosystem.

The team also hardened voice and vision pipelines by enhancing live half-duplex voice interactions through advanced acoustic echo cancellation primitives and activating the Kokoro TTS engine. On the performance side, agent startup latency was reduced via telemetry-driven boot breakdowns and persistent warm hosts for vision and computer-use tasks. Codebase stability was further improved by enforcing strict type-checking and refactoring Turbo build tasks for better cache efficiency.

Several pull requests are currently in progress, including work to filter chat document retrieval by requester access context, UI fixes for memory-type badge styling and Storybook mock providers, CI improvements to fail Hetzner end-to-end tests on invalid cloud authentication, cloud test contracts for AI pricing provider outage resilience, and hardening of the desktop shell and view applications.
